geocoding.note: Points are positioned at city/metro centroids, not exact incident coordinates. The FAA public records contain only date, state, city, and a narrative — no coordinates.

proximity.note: Each sighting's nd is the great-circle distance (nm) from its city/metro centroid to the nearest military-installation boundary; nb is that base's id. Hotspots are computed client-side over the selected time window.

Summary: DA40, maneuvering south of BNA while receiving VFR flight following, reported an UAS event to the DRE controller. LEOS REPORTED UAS WAS OPERATED OVER THE STADIUM AND REACHED POSSIBLE MAX ALTITUDE OF 150 FEET.


Summary: UNAUTHORIZTED UAS IN CLASS - B AT NIGHT Received a drone notification, with the pilot near 10th St & Phillies Dr. Command Center called 911 and responded to the scene. Found the pilot in a vehicle next to the Peco Substation, who was controlling the drone at this point over Lincoln Financial Field. Informed the pilot that this was a no-drone area and that he had to land the drone, which he brought back to his vehicle. Pilot showed me his expired FAA pilot registration, but he had not filed a flight plan for his flight. Stated he was from out of town, didn't know he couldn't fly in the area. FYI, the program does not show the height the drone reached, but it was high enough to clear the LFF north endzone stands and the CBP admin building. (+150FT)

Summary: Received a drone notification about a drone being flown from the southeast corner of Lot D towards Lincoln Financial Field (LFF). Contacted Well Fargo Center (WFC) Security about the pilot in their parking lot and contacted LFF Security about the drone over their stadium. The drone reached a height as high as 185 meters while over LFF. The drone was in the air over LFF as a helicopter for the Sixers was leaving the Lot N Helipad. The drone was airborne for 19 minutes and proceeded over the lots around WFC, LFF, & Jetro, LFF itself, and I-95 before the pilot, who was sitting in his vehicle, was forced to land by WFC & LFF Security and Philadelphia Police. Pilot information was provided by LFF Security.

Summary: At approximately 1615Z, a flight of 2 Canadian Snowbirds were in the right break near the approach end of runway 20. At that moment a drone was observed by the tower approximately 1000 feet north of the tower, about 1000 feet west of the extended centerline of runway 20 at approximately 150 feet AGL. UAS activity was issued to the Snowbird flight and it was observed that the drone was not an eminent threat to the aircraft. The flight of 2 were allowed to continue and land. ATM stepped out on the cat-walk and yelled at the operator to land the drone. Which he did immediately. The airport authority was called and they responded to the site of the operation. Sheriff was notified, but when operator landed the drone and left, the airport authority cancelled their call. Since that time, has provided me with a copy of his COA. This is the first time I've seen it. He was operating in a zero grid from the UASFM. COA and information attached.
